title: Harmony Patching

must_not_own_when: request is about assembly parsing or UI

solves: intercepting Unity game behavior through small, reviewable runtime hooks
turning discovered game methods into internal mod events without coupling features to patch classes
handling missing or changed targets through diagnostics and graceful degradation
does_not_solve: finding target methods without assembly analysis
loader startup and shutdown policy by itself

core_principles: A patch is an interception boundary, not a feature module.
Patch the smallest semantic lifecycle anchor that gives the needed payload.
Prefer postfix observation when the original game behavior should remain authoritative.
Use prefix only when state must be captured before mutation or execution must be guarded.
Use transpilers only when prefix/postfix/finalizer cannot express the requirement safely.
Every patch should report whether it is active.
constraints: Do not put UI, persistence, business rules, or large state machines inside patch methods.
Do not patch broad update loops when a semantic lifecycle method exists.
Do not assume target type or method presence across game versions.
Unpatch explicitly during shutdown when the loader/runtime supports teardown.
Keep patch payloads minimal and hand them to handlers, events, or runtime modules.
decision_rules: If the target method was not confirmed by dump or assembly reading, attach assembly-reading before implementing the patch.
If the patch only needs to observe completion, choose postfix.
If the patch needs pre-mutation state, choose prefix and pass only the required values to a handler.
If the feature needs many game internals after interception, create a reflection probe instead of expanding the patch method.
If a patch can be absent without breaking the whole mod, expose an unavailable diagnostic state and disable only dependent features.

preferred_patterns: thin patch class that locates, applies, removes, and reports the hook
separate hook handler for interpretation and event publication
central hook registration module with hook state snapshots
patch diagnostics that include target description and enabled state
runtime events and facades downstream from patches
avoid_patterns: feature logic directly inside prefix or postfix methods
silent failure when target lookup fails
patching every nearby method to discover behavior at runtime
unbounded reflection inside hot patch paths

recipes.name: Add a thin lifecycle patch
recipes.when: Use when a decompiled game method marks a useful lifecycle transition such as object loaded, race started, loading finished, or menu opened.
steps: Confirm exact target type, method name, and signature from assembly analysis or dump output.
Create a patch class that only resolves the target and installs prefix/postfix/finalizer.
Forward the minimal runtime payload to a dedicated handler.
Publish an internal event or update a lifecycle snapshot from the handler.
Define fallback behavior when the target type or method is missing.

recipes.name: Diagnose a broken hook after a game update
recipes.when: Use when a previously working patch no longer fires or target lookup fails.
steps: Check hook diagnostics to confirm whether the target type and method were found.
Compare current dump signatures against the recorded integration surface map.
Identify whether the target was renamed, overloaded, moved, removed, or delayed by lifecycle changes.
Update the integration map before changing patch code.
Keep dependent features disabled until the hook is confirmed active again.

pitfalls: Using a patch class as a convenient place for all feature behavior.
Choosing a fragile target because it is easy to find rather than semantically correct.
Ignoring patch ordering and existing patch owners when sharing a game method.
Failing to unpatch or mark disabled hooks during shutdown.
Letting a missing patch crash unrelated modules.
review_checklist: Is the target method exact and documented?
Is feature logic outside the patch class?
Does failure to find the target produce diagnostics?
Is there a clear fallback or feature-disable path?

references.title: Harmony Patching
references.interpretation: Use this first for patch selection, thin patch structure, and review expectations.
references.why: It encodes the domain policy that Harmony is an interception layer, not the mod architecture.

references.title: Runtime Integration
references.interpretation: Use this to place patches inside the larger runtime, event, probe, and facade architecture.
references.why: It connects Harmony hooks to stable mod runtime contracts.

references.title: Diagnostics And Readiness
references.interpretation: Use when patch availability should gate runtime features or expose health state.
references.why: It defines the readiness and diagnostic expectations around fragile integrations.
